title: Sago Pudding
excerpt: Ingredients 250 ml sago 1 litre milk 1 piece of stick cinnamon 30 ml butter 4 eggs, separated (keep the egg whites) 100 ml sugar 5 ml vanilla essence 3 ml salt apricot jam to taste 2 extra egg whites Method Soak the sago in the milk – if you don't do this, it's …
html:
Ingredients

  • 250 ml sago
  • 1 litre milk
  • 1 piece of stick cinnamon
  • 30 ml butter
  • 4 eggs, separated (keep the egg whites)
  • 100 ml sugar
  • 5 ml vanilla essence
  • 3 ml salt
  • apricot jam to taste
  • 2 extra egg whites

Method

Soak the sago in the milk – if you don't do this, it's no problem – you just cook it a bit longer.

Place the milk, sago and cinnamon stick in a pot and cook until the sago turns translucent. Watch out for burning! Keep the heat low and stir regularly. Stir in the butter and remove from the heat.

Whisk the egg yolks, half the sugar, vanilla essence and salt together and stir this into the sago mixture. Spoon the mixture into an ovenproof dish and dot it with apricot jam to taste.

In a clean bowl, whisk all 6 egg whites until almost stiff. Now add the remaining sugar bit by bit while you keep whisking until all the sugar has been whisked in. Spoon this on top of the sago mixture and bake in a preheated oven for 20 minutes at 160ºC and then for another 15 minutes at 190ºC.

Serves 6 – 8 portions with naartjie-flavoured custard.

Easy!
